comment: The words are interchangeable and I disagree it is helpful to send people to a subsection many page lengths down when they would probably much rather start out at the top of the article.  This has been discussed previously on talk page but I think [[Talk:Cannabis (drug)]] would probably be a better place to discuss it that would get more attention if you would like to pursue it.
Should redirect to the subsection about marijuana
redirect to entire page, not a subsection
Reverted to revision 606260854 by [[Special:Contributions/Dennis Brown|Dennis Brown]] ([[User talk:Dennis Brown|talk]]): Don't start an edit war during an RFC.  See [[WP:BRD]]. ([[WP:TW|TW]])
Undid revision 606260854 by [[Special:Contributions/Dennis Brown|Dennis Brown]] ([[User talk:Dennis Brown|talk]])
There was plenty of opposition, moving this way is completely improper.  Reverting.
Combined subsections; etymology and discussion of the word itself are the same thing.
Reworded lead to link to parent article and to clarify the nature of the hemp plant
Male buds can be smoked too
merged content from [[Marijuana (word)]]; created Marijuana article (ref. to dried, smoked variant of plant)
If we're undoing back to a redirect, let's undo back to [[Cannabis (drug)]].  This is what it was when the [[WP:RM]] debate started and what it's been through most of its history.
best wait until the move review is closed, at this point it seems to be trending to overturning the close
every policy or guideline cited in the move request was in favor of a move.
non-admin close, best have that reviewed
first and foremost to the plant, and then, at times, to the drug use of the plant

It should redirect to the drug
add *[[Legality of cannabis]] refers to marijuana and the law

moved [[Marijuana]] to [[Marijuana (disambiguation)]]: To allow primary topic redirect
